lesson.menu_desc: Work Emails: linking words and formal register -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 9 -- Email Essentials -- Work Emails
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Aline Sberci | Aula 9 -- Email Essentials

listenings.text: Hi Aline, this is Tom from the project team. I just sent you an important email, so please check your inbox. The subject is Monthly Report, and the attachment is the file with all the numbers. I am writing because the client needs our update before Friday. Please read it, and also forward it to your manager. If you have a question, just reply to me directly. I tried to keep the email short and polite. Thank you for your help, and talk soon.

This is a quick note to myself before I write my email. First, I choose a clear subject, so the recipient knows the topic. Then I start with a polite greeting, because first impressions matter. In the body, I write a short request, and I add the attachment with the report. I use simple linking words like and, but, and so to connect my ideas. Before I send, I read my draft one more time and check for mistakes. Finally, I close with Best regards, and I reply quickly to any answer. Writing a professional email is my new goal, and I feel ready.

extra_audio.text: First, Aline writes a clear subject, so the recipient knows the topic. Then she starts with a polite greeting. After that, she writes a short request in the body and adds the attachment. Next, she reads her draft and checks for mistakes. Finally, she closes with Best regards and sends the email.
